title: -Contract: African American/Black Professional Community Network for Project Sankofa

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any
amendments with African American/Black Professional Community Network (AABPN)
with Healthy Black Families as the fiscal sponsor for the African American Holistic
Resource Center, Project Sankofa, which will be funded through the State of California
Mental Health Services Act (MHSA) Community Services and Supports funds, for a not
to exceed amount of $300,000, through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding in the amount of $300,000 provided from the State of California Mental Health
Services Act Fund 315 is available in the Adopted FY26 budget.
sections.current_situation:
Per the City Council approved Mental Health Services Act (MHSA) FY24-FY26 Three
Year Program and Expenditure Plan, funding in the amount of $300,000 was allocated
to support the African American Holistic Resource Center (AAHRC), Project Sankofa.
The AABPN is the community organization that will implement Project Sankofa, which
will provide the following with this funding:
 Culturally relevant consultation and outreach in Berkeley schools and youth
programs with a goal of 50 students receiving at least 2 sessions.
 Training & Capacity Building providing culturally grounded sessions for youth,
caregivers, and educators that will deliver monthly training to a group of 20 youth.
 Digital Media Campaign & Civic Engagement for Youth to shift perceptions on
mental health through relevant social media and an online magazine. Three (3)
youth and young adult interns will be tasked to create bi-weekly posts, 1 virtual
town hall and additional online articles.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4

 Healing Circles & Forums providing community healing spaces with meals,
storytelling, and resource-sharing that will deliver community-based healing
spaces with meals, storytelling, and resource-sharing including an in-person
Juneteenth event and online forum for Black History month.
 Community Print & Digital Distribution providing the development of culturally
specific wellness materials for the community with input from the youth. Flyers,
brochures, and a website for the community with dedicated input and
engagement from youth will be developed and made available.
Project Sankofa will leverage community partnerships to assist with implementation,
training, content development, and direct community engagement. These par
sections.background:
The State of California provides MHSA funding for local mental health services and
supports. One of the annually occurring MHSA funding components is Community
Services & Supports (CSS). MHSA CSS funds are primarily to be utilized for treatment
services and supports for Severely Mentally Ill Adults and Seriously Emotionally
Disturbed Children and Youth, and for general system development.
To utilize MHSA funds, stakeholder informed Three Year Plans and Annual Updates
that outline how funds are to be utilized are required to be developed and locally
approved. Per Resolution No. 70,990-N.S., the City Council approved the MHSA FY24-
FY26 Three Year Program and Expenditure Plan, which allocated funding for the
AAHRC Project Sankofa.
The AAHRC will be a state-of-the-art resource center where culturally congruent
services will be provided for African Americans to obtain the support they need to
decrease inequities and disparities and build community. The AAHRC will be comprised
of various service components. Project Sankofa is one of the services that is being
introduced before the official opening of the AAHRC in Berkeley.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this project.

sections.rationale:
The execution of this contract will ensure that once opened, the AAHRC will be home to
new mental health and wellness services for the African American community in
Berkeley.
sections.alternatives:
As funding for the AAHRC Project Sankofa was included in the City Council approved
MHSA FY24-26 Three Year Plan, and Healthy Black Families has been designated as
the fiscal sponsor of this project, there were no other alternative actions considered.
CONTACT PERSON
Tasha Tervalon, Assistant to the City Manager, (510) 981-5347

1: Resolution
Page 3

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: AFRICAN AMERICAN/BLACK PROFESSIONAL COMMUNITY
NETWORK FOR PROJECT SANKOFA
WHEREAS, the City’s Department of Health, Housing and Community Services, Mental
Health Division, receives Mental Health Services Act (MHSA) Community Services and
Supports (CSS) funding on an annual basis to provide services and supports for Severely
Mentally Ill Adults and Seriously Emotionally Disturbed Children and Youth, and for
general system development; and
WHEREAS, in order to utilize MHSA CSS Funds, the Mental Health Division must have
a stakeholder informed, locally approved Three Year Plan or Annual Update outlining how
funds will be allocated in place; and
WHEREAS, per Resolution No. 70,990-N.S., City Council approved the MHSA FY24-
FY26 Three Year Program and Expenditure Plan; and
WHEREAS, the City Council approved MHSA FY24-FY26 Three Year Program and
Expenditure Plan included funding for the African American Holistic Resource Center for
the City to work with the African American/Black Professional Community Network to
implement Project Sankofa; and
WHEREAS, Project Sankofa will provide mental health and wellness services,
workshops, healing circles, community engagement activities, and an online social media
campaign for African Americans in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, the African American Holistic Resource Center community-led steering
committee has designated Healthy Black Families as the fiscal sponsor for Project
Sankofa; and
WHEREAS, the City Manager’s Office will execute and monitor this contract; and
WHEREAS, funding in the amount of $300,000 is available in the Adopted FY26 Budget
in the Mental Health Services Act Fund 315; and
WHEREAS, MHSA CSS funds in the amount of $300,000 will be transferred to the City
Manager’s Office to execute and provide oversight of this contract.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is hereby authorized to execut
